stem: In right triangle ABC, the angle at C is a right angle. The length of side BC is 8 and the length of the hypotenuse AB is 17. What is the value of the sine of angle A?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. In a right triangle, the sine of an acute angle is the ratio of the length of the side opposite that angle to the length of the hypotenuse. Side BC is opposite angle A and has length 8, and the hypotenuse AB has length 17. So the sine of angle A equals 8 over 17.

Choice A is incorrect: 1 over 17 has no meaningful interpretation here.

Choice B is incorrect: 15 over 17 is the cosine of angle A.

Choice D is incorrect: 17 over 8 is the reciprocal of the sine.
